prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear sweetie, a CPU is kind of like the brain of the computer—though I must admit, I still don’t quite understand how it works myself! It’s the little part that makes all the decisions and tells the rest of the machine what to do, just like how I used to make decisions for the family, remember? When you click something or type a letter, the CPU is what says, “Ah yes, that’s what we’re supposed to do now!” It’s usually a small, shiny chip tucked inside the computer’s box. Even though I don’t use one much these days, I know it’s important because without it, the whole thing wouldn’t know what to do!
